title: Welcome!
text:
My name is Jenna Baljunas, and I study community ecology!

Currently, I am a PhD student in the Spatial and Community Ecology (SpaCE) Lab at Michigan State University and a part of the Department of Integrative Biology and Ecology, Evolution, and Behavior program.

  
  







I am interested in how environmental change affects community structure and function, particularly in the tropics.

The interactions between species are among the fundamental drivers of tropical biodiversity. For my dissertation research, I am leveraging species interaction networks using occurrence, interaction, and trait data to quantify community change along a 2,900m elevational gradient in Costa Rica.
